content:
Hello there.


You've removed all tasks from the calendar and calendar itself. You can restore this calendar ; Go to View> Calendar> Show Calendar panel. Hereby you can restore that calendar.

Recent issue If you deleted the data and you didn't close thunderbird. Press ctrl + Z for Win/Linux. Or press cmd + Z to undo the deleted task. But, did you use or had a backup at that time?

Is there a backup You've deleted all the tasks, okay. What calendar was there that you used there and erased in mistake? Google calendar or lightning? Are you using Windows or another one? You can find the "calendar data" folder in the profile folder when you close Thunderbird. Is it there? You can import these into the software if it s missing or deleted , as you wrote.

Local.sqlite If you saved these data or you do have a backup of those tasks / data. You can copy the local.sqlite from that backup device and put them back in the calendar data folder. This replaces the old empty space / file. Restart Thunderbird.
creator: Googlethunderbird.

content:
Please post the following information: In Thunderbird

  • Help > Troubleshooting Information
  • scroll down to 'Mail and News Accounts'
  • Copy all the data and paste it into this forum question.

SMTP - If you use Port:

  • port 465 then use 'Connection SEcurity: 'SSL/TLS'
  • port 587 then use 'Connection SEcurity: 'STARTTLS'

Check the Authentication is also set as: Authentication Method: Normal Password

On computer check you have the following: Firewall: Access Firewall - it might be the default on computer or controlled by an Anti-Virus prgram. Set up the Thunderbird program as 'allowed' so nothing gets blocked. Do not just switch it off because if controlled by an Anti-Virus then the default one on Windows takes over. Confirm Thunderbird is set up as allowed program.

VPN: Check computer for a VPN. Some Anti-Virus products now offer a VPN and I have come across some people who did not realise one had been enabled. Switch off VPN. Many servers do not like VPN as it atempts to hide your IP address, so server decides you might be hacker so it auto blocks access. Confirm VPN is switched off

When you ask a question, it's important to add information to suppliment the question.

What OS do you use? Windows , MAC, Linux ? What version of Thunderbird are you using ? Are you talking about a Pop or Imap mail account ? What server settings are you using for incoming and outgoing ? You can upload images that show that data found in Account Settings OR you can also find it in Help > TRoubleshooting Information - scroll down to Mail and News Accounts section - post image or copy and paste the data.

What error message do you get when you try to 'Get Messages' or Send email ? We need it word for word verbatim or upload an image showing the error.

In the meantime check the following because they could be blocking access:

On computer check you have the following: Firewall: Access Firewall - it might be the default on computer or controlled by an Anti-Virus prgram. Set up the Thunderbird program as 'allowed' so nothing gets blocked. Do not just switch it off because if controlled by an Anti-Virus then the default one on Windows takes over. Confirm Thunderbird is set up as allowed program.

VPN: Check computer for a VPN. Some Anti-Virus products now offer a VPN and I have come across some people who did not realise one had been enabled. Switch off VPN. Many servers do not like VPN as it atempts to hide your IP address, so server decides you might be hacker so it auto blocks access. Confirm VPN is switched off

content:
Check Help Article as there are recent changes:

  • https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/thunderbird-and-yahoo

Please check the following which has always applied but ensure nothing has altered. On computer: Firewall: Access Firewall - it might be the defaulton computer or controlled by an Anti-Virus prgram. Set up Thunderbird program as 'allowed' so nothing gets blocked. Confirm Thunderbird is set up as allowed program.

VPN: Check computer for a VPN. Some Anti-Virus products now offer a VPN and I have come across some people who did not realise one had been enabled. Switch off VPN (or create split tunnelling and set it up for Thunderbird to use). Many servers do not like VPN as it atempts to hide your IP address, so server decides you might be hacker so it auto blocks access. Confirm VPN is switched off

Localhost: Do you have anything using the localhost? People who manage websites may typically use eg: Apache Xampp. You need to switch of any program using the localhost whilst setting up gmail. You can enable it again afterwards. I had to switch off Apache Xampp. Confirm nothing is using localhost.

In Thunderbird

  • Settings > Privacy & Security
  • Web Content
  • Select 'Accept cookies from sites'
  • Click on 'Exceptions' button
  • Enter data such as: yahoo.com, aol.com or att.com.
  • Click on 'Allow
  • Click on 'Save Changes'

Whilst in that area - scroll to Passwords section

  • click on 'Saved Passwords'
  • If you see any mention of the account/username - select the relevant lines and click on 'Remove'. You may have an oauth line and/or imap line and possibly smtp line.
  • Click on Close

In 'Account Settings' check both incoming and outgoing 'Authentication Method' is set as 'Oauth2'

then Exit Thunderbird - wait a few moments for background processes to complete - restart Thunderbird.

You should get asked for password by yahoo and then asked to allow Thunderbird.
creator: Toad-Hall

content:
Per poter inquadrare il problema prova a fornire queste informazioni:

  1. Versione di Thunderbird
  2. Versione del sistema operativo
  3. Provider delle caselle email (Gmail, Libero, Hotmail, etc.)
  4. Tipologia di account (POP3, IMAP)
  5. Estensioni installate
  6. Messaggi di errore che vengono visualizzati durante l'utilizzo di Thunderbird
  7. Software di sicurezza eventualmente presenti (Antivirus e simili)
  8. Screenshot del problema (se utile a chiarire il contesto)

content:
The latest error message i have received is "Unable to log in at server. Probably wrong configuration, username or password"

As per your Troubleshooting Information there is no Gmail account in your Thunderbird profile. Therefore, are you saying you do get the error when attempting to re-create the Gmail account in Thunderbird?

If so, make sure to allow cookies in Thunderbird. Don't use a VPN when attempting to authenticate. Also make sure there is no local web server running on your computer or any other service listening to TCP port 80. To check this open a command prompt, and run this command: 

netstat -an | findstr LISTEN | findstr ":80[^0-9]"

If you are still stuck, please send screenshots illustrating the problem.

So the correct server settings are found eventually, but you fail later on in the process. Please confirm cookies are allowed in Thunderbird, and that you do not use a VPN.

Antivirus: Kaspersky Total Security

If you still fail, try to start Windows 10 in safe mode with networking enabled.

Windows safe mode disables anti-virus software. This is a troubleshooting step, not a solution.

content:
Please work throught this as it has sorted things for many people using gmail.

Running computer as normal.

1. Firewall: Access Firewall - it might be the default on computer or controlled by an Anti-Virus prgram. Set up Thunderbird program as 'allowed' so nothing gets blocked.

  • Confirm Thunderbird is set up as allowed program.

2. VPN: Check computer for a VPN. Some Anti-Virus products now offer a VPN and I have come across some peopl who did not realise one had been enabled. Switch off VPN (or create split tunnelling and set it up for Thunderbird to use). Gmail does not like VPN as it atempts to hide your IP address, so gmail decides you might be hacker so it auto blocks access.

  • Confirm VPN is switched off

3. Localhost: Do you have anything using the localhost? People who manage websites may typically use eg: Apache Xampp. You need to switch of any program using the localhost whilst setting up gmail. You can enable it again afterwards. I had to switch off Apache Xampp.

  • Confirm nothing is using localhost.

4. In Thunderbird

  • Settings > Privacy & Security
  • Web Content
  • Select 'Accept cookies from sites'
  • Click on 'Exceptions' button
  • Enter this : https://accounts.google.com
  • Click on 'Allow
  • Click on 'Save Changes'

If you had previous set up gmail and removed the account then do this:

  • Settings > Privacy & Security
  • scroll to Passwords section
  • click on 'Saved Passwords'
  • If you see any mention of the account you are trying to set up - select the relevant lines and click on 'Remove'
  • Click on Close

Switch off the following as it's still experimental.

  • Settings > General
  • Account Hub
  • uncheck this checkbox: 'Create accounts in the new Account Hub'

5. Now create the mail account

  • Menu app > New Account > Email
  • Enter name
  • Enter gmail email address
  • Enter password
  • Select checkbox to remember password.
  • Click on 'Continue'

Thunderbird by default will look for the IMAP server settings. They should be:

  • Server name: imap.gmail.com
  • Port: 993
  • Connection Security: SSL/TLS
  • Authentication Method: OAuth2
  • Username = full gmail email address
  • Password is the same one you use to access webmail

Outgoing SMTP:

  • Server name: smtp.gmail.com
  • Port: 465
  • Connection Security: SSL/TLS
  • Authentication Method: OAuth2
  • Username = full gmail email address
  • Password is the same one you use to access webmail

Assuming all is ok click on 'Done'

You should then get the gmail pop up browser type window. It will ask you to confirm you really are you and the then ask if you want to allow Thunderbird access to server. You need to allow access.

Then an OAuth token gets created and stored in the same place as all other passwords.

Everything working fine now. Many thanks for your help and for Chris for all his efforts.

creator: wsmwk

content:
Wayne Mery said
I don't expect this going to be related to TWC/spectrum.

It only occurs with the TWC/Spectrum accounts and the problem clears by selecting another folder then returning to the Unified Inbox.

Based on the variety of other problems that multiple users have reported between Thunderbird and Spectrum (especially the legacy accounts), I strongly suspect that the issue is specific to the combination of Thunderbird together with Spectrum's legacy IMAP servers.

All the problems that I have observed here and most of the ones I have read of others having between Thunderbird and Spectrum might be explained by handshake issues between the two systems. Such handshake issues could be caused by timing and/or minor differences in the codes being exchanged.

This isn't a firm diagnosis (which would require network analysis at the server) but just an educated guess.

content: Thanks you, please I just want to know how can I make an automatic redirection from ThunderBird to Proton Mail. That’s all… maybe somebody know how to do that ?
creator: morgane.aquarelle

content: Please understand that Thunderbird is an email client, while Proton Mail is an email provider. If Proton Mail is your email provider, then you're already there. Thunderbird's function is to provide a management tool for composing, filing, retaining email addresses. To clarify the data flow, - email arrives from an email host to thunderbird - thunderbird then provides the tools to redirect, file, delete, forward the message. You can create a filter to redirect all messages from the incoming email host to an account on Proton Mail, but that doesn't seem to do what you want. Wayne asked for details so that someone here may be able to assist. Even if you want to leave thunderbird, it appears you still need assistance. For example, if you have an IMAP account, then you can just remove thunderbird and use Proton Mail. Done. But if you have a POP account, you may want to retain those messages.

Buenos día: Pues no mejoró en absoluto..... sigue tardando entre 15 y 18 segundos en enviar un simple email. De hecho acabo de probar a acceder con los complementos desactivados y restaurar las barras de herramientas y nada!!

Hemos optado por una solución temporal, mientras no encuentre la forma de resolverlo. Es un "truco" que no lo soluciona pero hace que moleste menos la espera:

Herramientas->Ajustes->General-> Abajo de todo hay un boton "Editor de configuracion..." Escribir: mailnews.sendInBackground Y cambio su valor de False a True (dando doble click sobre el false) y reinicio Thunderbird. Esto hace que no se quede el programa en "enviando..." y procede a enviar el email desde el background permitiendo seguir usando el programa. Todo esto parece derivar de las últimas actualizaciones de Windows o Thunderbird desde diciembre (no puedo decir una fecha exacta), yo me inclino por algo que han cambiado en Windows y que provoca ese lentitud anormal, aunque no consigo ver nada anormal en el administrador de tareas.

I stumbled on that list and can not find it for the life of me. Finally, when you say login online, who are you talking about? Sorry, I am so slow on this. It's been a pain. It might eventually force me away from Thunderbird. The address in question is, as far as I can tell perfectly legitimate, and has been in use for several years. Thank you so much for your time and consideration. Michael Tad Parks+

Wow. Outlook Express. That comment brings back many good memories. :) Okay, regarding online, I was suggesting that you log onto the website that maintains your email address. For example, if you have a Gmail email address, you could access the account by going to gmail.com with your browser. The suggestion to try online is to see if your email provider is setting that email as spam. If so, the message will be spam before Thunderbird receives it.

On filters, you can do that at tools>message filters. Set the filter to search for FROM contains and specify the email address there. For action, I suggest marking it as not spam. In upper left part of that screen is the place to set whether to run the filter before or after the spam check. I would try setting to AFTER the spam check.
creator: davidsk

content: Yes, you can if you have created a so-called "mailing list" in one of your Thunderbird address books. To create a new mailing list open the Address Book tab, select an address book and click on the little "group" symbol at the top of the address books pane to open a box "New Mailing List". Give it a name +/- a description, then click OK to create the mailing list which will be displayed in the left pane like a sub-folder of the address book. You don't need to add email addresses in this box right now: instead open any of your address books and add the contacts you want to be included in your mailing list via drag & drop.... To forward a message to this mailing list, just click the Forward button and type the name/nickname of your mailing list in the To (or Cc or Bcc) field in the Write window.
creator: Mapenzi

content:
There are lines or different colors between various sections of the interface, including the message list and message page, and in Settings, as you can see in the attached images. There are also scroll bars, so you should be able to scroll no matter what.

You're right, as of last year, you can't set a high-contrast theme within Thunderbird, but it does respect the system setting in that regard.

There is an unofficial hack to modify the interface with the use of a userchrome.css file. It's beyond the scope of this forum, but there are discussions of it around the Internet. Incorrect use of it can break the interface (but it's easy to revert) and future updates may break its effectiveness.
creator: rednoise

content:
A userChrome.css file is useful for making specific interface changes. For example, your first issue can be addressed (I think) by assigning a background colour to the header pane (see picture). For other elements, post a screenshot that exactly points out the desired changes.

Many themes can be found on this forum:

https://www.reddit.com/r/Thunderbird/comments/1qc4uv0/happy_new_year_and_happy_new_theme/

creator: MarjaE

content:
To set the background colour of the header pane, add this to userChrome.css: 

.message-header-container { background-color: lightblue !important; }


To set colors of the scrollbars, add this to userChrome.css and userContent.css: 

:root {      scrollbar-color: orange #87CEFA !important; }


As for the Settings tab, I can only find this for userContent.css: 

@-moz-document regexp("about.*") {    body {          background-color: lightblue  ;    } }


https://www.elevenforum.com/t/thunderbird-115-css-customization.16320/post-647931
creator: sfhowes
